diagrams.title: Computer System Organization Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the basic components of a computer system and their interconnections through the system bus.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how hardware components are organized and communicate

diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the memory hierarchy from fastest/smallest to slowest/largest capacity.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the trade-off between speed, capacity, and cost in memory design

diagrams.title: Interrupt Processing Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step flow of how an interrupt is handled by the CPU.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding asynchronous event handling in computer systems

diagrams.title: I/O Techniques Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows the three I/O techniques and their data flow patterns.
elements: DMA: Device ↔ Memory (direct transfer with CPU coordination)
diagrams.purpose: Comparing different approaches to handling input/output operations
